design: content-matched real/clone control extended to Seed-VC (self-conversion: content AND prosody matched) and GPT-SoVITS v4 (ASR text-matched); protocol identical to content_matched_control.py
notes: Seed-VC control uses self-conversion: the held-out real segment itself is the VC source, so content and prosody are both matched. If separability persists, the covariate-shift reading is even stronger than for the text-only-matched Irodori control.

Reference (b) uses the original content-unmatched clones of the same synthesizer restricted to the same speakers, same real segments, same separability protocol (logreg on centered+L2-normed embeddings, GroupKFold by speaker).
caveats: Seed-VC self-conversion also removes prosody/style mismatch, so (a) vs (b) for Seed-VC bounds content+prosody jointly, not content alone.
GPT-SoVITS matching is approximate: ASR (large-v3) + TTS errors upper-bound the mismatch (see CER block); prompt transcription uses the same run_gptsovits_ver.py recipe (whisper of ref_wavs[0]).
Within-synthesizer comparisons only; numbers are not directly comparable to the all-synthesizer separability in the main text.

meta.method: seed-vc v2 self-conversion (source = held-out real segment, prompt = target's standard concatenated reference)
gpt-sovits v4 zero-shot, same recipe as run_gptsovits_ver.py, text = faster-whisper large-v3 transcript of held-out real segment
